class.name: GridMapSingleton
class.brief_description: A singleton managing the logical state of a gameplay grid synchronized with a TileMapLayer.
class.description: The class automatically initializes a 2D matrix of grid cells by synchronizing with a neighboring [TileMapLayer] node (located at the hardcoded path [code]"../TileMapLayer"[/code]). It reads custom tile data ([code]"walkable"[/code]), tracks objects occupying the grid (Occupants), and handles ground hazards/zones (Effects), seamlessly translating global grid coordinates into internal matrix indices.

method.name: clear_occupant
method.description: Clears the occupant data at the specified global grid cell [param p_cell], making the cell vacant.

method.name: get_effect
method.description: Returns the ground effect node located at the global grid cell [param p_cell]. Returns [code]null[/code] if there is no effect or if the coordinates are out of bounds.

method.name: get_occupant
method.description: Returns the node (e.g., a character or obstacle) occupying the global grid cell [param p_cell]. Returns [code]null[/code] if the cell is empty. Automatically cleans up stale references if the occupying object was previously destroyed.

method.name: is_tile_walkable
method.description: Checks whether the global grid cell [param p_cell] can be traversed. A cell is considered walkable if it is within map boundaries, its tile is not flagged as a wall (has [code]walkable == true[/code]), and it is not currently occupied by another node ([method get_occupant]).

method.name: on_cell_entered
method.description: Triggered when an entity [param p_node] enters the global grid cell [param p_cell]. If a ground effect is present at these coordinates, this method automatically attempts to call its [code]apply_effect(p_node)[/code] function via duck-typing.

method.name: set_occupant
method.description: Attempts to assign the node [param p_node] as the occupant of the global grid cell [param p_cell]. Returns [code]true[/code] if successful. Returns [code]false[/code] if the cell is already occupied by a different node or if the coordinates are out of bounds.
